questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the procedure for an officer who wishes to resign from the service?
options: They must apply for a leave of absence.
They must submit their resignation letter to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must give one month's notice of their intention to resign.
They must give three months' notice
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must give one month's notice of their intention to resign.). Rule 020904 states that an officer who wishes to resign must give "a month's notice."

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines the condition regarding transfers or lateral conversions into the Foreign Service?
options: It is not allowed above GL 10 due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.
It is a common practice for promotions.
It is encouraged for experienced staff from other ministries.
It is permissible with special approval from the President.3.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It is not allowed above GL 10 due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.). Due to the unique nature of the Foreign Service, lateral conversions or transfers into it are not permitted for officers above Grade Level

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines one of the conditions for a non-pensionable officer to be granted leave?
options: The leave is at the discretion of the Permanent Secretary. Q
They are entitled to leave in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve of the Rules.
They are entitled to annual leave as per the rules.
The rules do not provide for leave.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (They are entitled to leave in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve of the Rules.). Rule 021214 states that the person engaged "must be entitled to leave in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the role of the completed personal emolument form according to FR 1521?
options: To justify the officer's annual leave
To serve as the basis for opening the group registers, which must be audited before an officer is included in the payroll.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To serve as the basis for opening the group registers, which must be audited before an officer is included in the payroll.). Financial Regulation 1521(ii) states that the completed personal emolument form must be the basis for opening the group registers, which must be audited before any officer is included in the payroll.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: What happens if a Foreign Service Officer’s marriage to a foreigner is deemed not to be in the interest of the Service?
options: The officer will be demand to leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service11.
The officer will be suspended without disbursement.
The officer will be demoted to a lower grade level.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer will be demand to leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service11.). If the marriage to a foreigner is not in the best interest of the Service, the officer will be demand to either leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence of reproducing any part of the 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules without express approval or authorization?
options: It is subject to a minor fine. □
It is copyrighted, and reproduction is prohibited without express approval.
It necessitate only a verbal notification.
It is encouraged for wider dissemination.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (It is copyrighted, and reproduction is prohibited without express approval.). The Preamble states, "The 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules is copyrighted. Accordingly, no part of this publication must be reproduced without express approval or authorization by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation." 8

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines the minimum age requirement for eligibility for appointment into the Federal Public Service?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Not less than 18 years). Rule 020206(a) states that to be eligible for appointment, every applicant must be "not less than 18 years and not more than 50 years of age."

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: An officer wishing to transfer from one senior post to another within the Federal Public Service must have served for a minimum period of how many months?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (6 months). Rule 020307 states that an officer must have served for a minimum period of six months in his original post before seeking a transfer to another post.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the consequence for an officer whose office is no longer demand due to the abolition of office?
options: They will be redeployed to another office.
They will be offered a new appointment.
They will be compulsorily retired from the Service.
They will be placed on a leave of absence. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They will be compulsorily retired from the Service.). Rule 020907 states that an officer whose service is no longer necessitate due to the abolition of office "must be necessitate to leave the Service." Part 12: Mandatory Contribution to Pension Scheme

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What happens if a non-pensionable officer is compelled to resign due to ill health not caused by their own negligence?
options: The officer will receive a full month's salary.
The officer will not receive any salary.
The officer will receive a severance allowance. a
The officer must receive such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer must receive such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation.). Rule 021207 states that if an officer is compelled to resign due to ill health, "he must payment such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation or the date on which a certificate in that respect was issued.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence for an officer who fails to pass the compulsory confirmation examination within the stipulated period?
options: The officer is suspended without payment. □
The officer is demoted to a lower grade level.
The officer is granted an extension to retake the exam.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer's appointment is terminated.). Rule 030104 clearly states that failure to pass the examination within the specified time must result in the termination of the officer's appointment.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the condition for using the COMPRO result for promotion from a junior to a senior post?
options: The officer must be recommended by the Permanent Secretary. □
The officer must have served for a minimum of two years.
The officer must have passed the exam on their first attempt.
The officer must meet other eligibility criteria for the senior post.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer must meet other eligibility criteria for the senior post.). Rule 030502 outlines that the result is used as a prerequisite, but the officer must still meet other conditions for promotion.

questions.question: Within government administration, at what point should a refund of a deposit be made?
options: At the end of the financial year
As soon as it is due, provided that all necessary checks have been made
Only with the explicit approval of the Minister of Finance □
When there is sufficient balance in the deposit account
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (As soon as it is due, provided that all necessary checks have been made). Financial Regulation 1307 states that refunds of deposits will be made as soon as they are due, provided that all necessary checks and reconciliations have been completed.

questions.question: Within government administration, which role has the constitutional power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).). The FCSC is the constitutional body that has the power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: An officer can only claim to be acting in a superior post if which condition is met?
options: They have served in the post for more than 6 months.
Their Permanent Secretary gives a verbal approval. □
They have been asked to cover the schedule of the superior officer.
The acting appointment has been expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The acting appointment has been expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.). For an officer to be able to claim to be acting on a particular post, such an arrangement must be expressly approved in writing by the constituted authority.

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: The 2021 Edition of the Public Service Rules is a review of which previous edition?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (2008 Edition). The foreword mentions, "This edition of the Public Service Rules is therefore a review of the 2008 edition."

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service?
options: The date of assumption of duty 15
The first day of the month of appointment
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (1st January of every year). Rule 020104 establishes that the incremental date of an officer is the 1st of January every year, provided the officer has spent at least six months on the grade with good performance before enjoying the increment.

questions.question: which option best describes necessitate for a person who has previously been employed in public service and dismissed or called upon to resign?
options: They are eligible for reappointment immediately.
They are not eligible for reappointment. □
They must obtain the approval of the Commission's powers, and it should be obtained irrespective of any delegation of the Commission's powers.
They must obtain prior approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must obtain the approval of the Commission's powers, and it should be obtained irrespective of any delegation of the Commission's powers.). Rule 020207(i)(b) necessitate that any such applicant must obtain approval from the Commission's powers, "irrespective of any delegation."

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What document is demand for appointment to senior posts?
options: Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 1)
Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)). Rule 020208(a) specifies that all applicants for senior posts must submit a completed "Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)".

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What form should an officer newly appointed to a pensionable establishment use to provide their personal particulars?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Gen 60). Rule 020209 states that a newly appointed officer is demand to enter his/her personal particulars on a Staff document Form (Gen 60).

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the requirement for Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices regarding transfers on junior posts?
options: They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must not accept any staff seconded without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.
They must conduct an interview for the staff a.
They must notify the Federal Civil Service Commission immediately.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (They must not accept any staff seconded without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.). Rule 020507(j) states that "No Ministry must accept any staff seconded to it without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board."

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the requirement for MDAs regarding staff on transfer/secondment?
options: They must seek approval from the Federal Civil Service Commission for every transfer.
They must submit a report every six months.
They must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must sustain a list of all seconded staff. 3
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.). Rule 020509 states that MDAs "must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil 27 Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation on the number of staff on transfer/secondment." Part 8: Posting of Officers

questions.question: According to established rules, at what point can an acting appointment be made?
options: When an officer is being considered for promotion.
Where a particular duty post is not lower than SGL 14 and the substantive holder is on leave or otherwise not available for posting.
Only when a substantive post becomes vacant.
Only for a short period of time. a
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Where a particular duty post is not lower than SGL 14 and the substantive holder is on leave or otherwise not available for posting.). Rule 020701 specifies that an acting appointment is made when a particular duty 30 post not lower than SGL 14 becomes vacant because the substantive holder is on leave, secondment, or other similar circumstances.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the effective date of an acting appointment?
options: The date the FCSC is notified.
The first day of the month. □
The date of the gazette notice of the appointment.
The date on which the officer takes over substantively the duties and responsibilities of the post.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The date on which the officer takes over substantively the duties and responsibilities of the post.). Rule 020705 states that the date on which an acting appointment commences "will be indicated in the gazette notice authorizing the appointment" and takes effect when the officer takes over the duties and responsibilities65.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes the accountability of ministries regarding acting appointments?
options: They must conduct a fresh promotion exercise.
They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must give a written test to the officer. □
They must inform the Federal Civil Service Commission about the acting appointment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They must inform the Federal Civil Service Commission about the acting appointment.). Rule 020707 specifies that ministries "must be necessitate to notify the Federal Civil Service Commission when officers revert from acting appointments."

subcategories.name: Allowances, Pay & Benefits
subcategories.description: Allowances, pay administration, and benefits under PSR.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision governs payment of duty tour allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 130101 defines duty tour allowance as payment to cover expenses incurred by an officer on official assignment away from duty station.

questions.question: Which officer authorizes duty tour for a staff within the same station
questions.explanation: PSR 130102(a) empowers the Head of Department to approve duty tours within the same station.

questions.question: What is the official term for payment covering overnight accommodation while on duty outside headquarters
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 states that Estacode is payable to officers on duty outside Nigeria or for overnight official assignments.

questions.question: Responsibility allowance is payable to officers who:
options: Hold additional official duties beyond their schedule.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106 provides that responsibility allowance compensates officers who perform additional official responsibilities beyond their normal schedule.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule regulates acting allowance for officers performing duties of a higher post
questions.explanation: PSR 130107 defines acting allowance as compensation for officers temporarily performing functions of a higher position.

questions.question: An officer must act in a higher post for a minimum of how many days before qualifying for acting allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(b) specifies that acting allowance is payable only if the officer acts for not less than 21 consecutive days.

questions.question: Who approves acting appointments in the Federal Civil Service
questions.explanation: PSR 020707 and 130107(c) state that the Federal Civil Service Commission approves acting appointments in the Federal Civil Service.

questions.question: What is the purpose of hardship allowance as provided in **PSR** 2021
options: For officers in difficult terrains or conditions of service.
questions.explanation: PSR 130109 establishes hardship allowance for officers serving in locations with difficult terrain or adverse conditions.

questions.question: Transport allowance is intended to cover what
questions.explanation: PSR 130110 provides that transport allowance is granted to officers to cover cost of transportation from home to office and back.

questions.question: Who determines the rate of overtime allowance in the public service
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 stipulates that the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission determines overtime allowance rates for public servants.

questions.question: What **PSR** provision governs meal subsidy allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 130112 provides that meal subsidy allowance is payable to officers who are not provided with meals while on duty under certain conditions.

questions.question: Which allowance is payable to compensate officers working under hazardous conditions
questions.explanation: PSR 130113 states that hazard allowance is granted to officers working under dangerous or unhealthy conditions.

questions.question: What allowance may be paid to officers who use their private vehicles for official duty
questions.explanation: PSR 130114 allows payment of mileage allowance to officers who use private vehicles for official assignments with prior approval.

questions.question: When does acting allowance cease to be payable
options: When the officer resumes substantive post.
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(d) states that acting allowance ceases once the officer returns to his substantive post.

questions.question: Which authority determines local duty tour allowance rates within Nigeria
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: PSR 130101(b) provides that the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission determines local duty tour rates.

questions.question: Who approves overtime work schedules in **MDAs**'
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(b) requires that overtime work schedules be approved by the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office before implementation.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule governs payment of acting allowance to an officer on leave
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(e) provides that an officer on leave is not entitled to acting allowance during the period of absence.

questions.question: Which of the following allowances is taxable under the **PSR**'
questions.explanation: PSR 130106(c) provides that responsibility allowance is taxable as part of emoluments.

questions.question: An officer entitled to overtime pay cannot work more than how many hours of overtime per day
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(d) limits overtime to a maximum of 4 hours per day except in emergencies.

questions.question: Which allowance is paid to compensate officers for performing extra duties on weekends or public holidays
questions.explanation: PSR 130111 provides overtime allowance for extra work beyond normal hours, including weekends and holidays.

questions.question: Who authorizes the payment of duty tour allowance outside Nigeria
options: President through the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104 assigns authorization for foreign duty tour allowance to the President through OHCSF.

questions.question: What is the definition of salary according to **PSR** 2021
options: All cash payments received by an officer in a month.
Basic pay and all taxable allowances.
questions.explanation: PSR 140101 defines salary as the basic pay plus all taxable allowances due to an officer monthly.

questions.question: Salaries of officers are paid how frequently according to **PSR**'
questions.explanation: PSR 140102 stipulates that salaries are paid monthly in arrears on or before the last working day of each month.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, who is responsible for certifying payment vouchers for salary
questions.explanation: PSR 140104 assigns responsibility for certifying salary payment vouchers to the Director of Finance and Accounts.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule authorizes the granting of salary advances to officers
questions.explanation: PSR 140201 authorizes the granting of salary advances to officers under conditions specified by the Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Salary advances granted to officers must not exceed how many months of basic salary
questions.explanation: PSR 140201(b) limits salary advances to one month’s basic salary, recoverable within three months.

questions.question: Who approves the grant of salary advance in Ministries and Departments
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: PSR 140201(c) provides that salary advances are approved by the Accounting Officer—Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule governs payment of transport allowance during transfer of duty station
questions.explanation: PSR 130115 authorizes transport allowance for officers officially transferred to new duty stations.

questions.question: Who determines the rates for Estacode and duty tour allowances abroad
options: Office of the Head of Civil Service.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission with Presidential approval.
questions.explanation: PSR 130104(b) provides that NSIWC determines rates for foreign Estacode subject to Presidential approval.

questions.question: An officer is entitled to a passage allowance when:
options: He is posted on transfer involving relocation.
questions.explanation: PSR 130115(b) provides that passage allowance is payable for officers transferred to stations requiring relocation.

questions.question: What is the definition of ‘allowance’ under **PSR** 2021
options: Operationally, extra payment for all officers under normal controls.
Financial benefit to meet specific official expenditure or condition of service.
In practice, compulsory benefit payable monthly in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, bonus for outstanding performance under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 130100 defines allowance as a financial benefit paid to meet specific service-related expenses or conditions.

questions.question: What is the objective of responsibility allowance
options: To compensate for additional administrative burdens.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106(a) states that responsibility allowance compensates officers with administrative or supervisory burdens beyond normal schedule.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule provides for hardship allowance
questions.explanation: PSR 130109 provides for hardship allowance payable to officers serving in difficult terrain or adverse environmental conditions.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, what is the difference between hazard and hardship allowances
options: Hazard is for dangerous conditions; hardship is for difficult living conditions.
Hazard is payable monthly, hardship annually.
Hardship is taxable, hazard is not.
questions.explanation: PSR 130109–130113 differentiate hardship (for adverse terrain) and hazard (for dangerous work environments).

questions.question: Overtime allowance shall not be paid to officers on which grade levels
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(c) states that officers on GL.07 and above are not entitled to overtime allowance.

questions.question: Which allowance is payable to cover minor operational expenses during official travel
questions.explanation: PSR 130101 describes duty tour allowance as payment for incidental and operational expenses during official assignments.

questions.question: Payment of any allowance not recognized by **PSR** requires approval from which body
options: Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130100(c) requires NSIWC approval before introducing or varying any allowance not already recognized in PSR.

questions.question: How long may an officer act in a higher position before confirmation or replacement is required
questions.explanation: PSR 130107(f) limits acting appointments to six months before confirmation or substitution.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule regulates loan advances to officers
questions.explanation: PSR 140205 allows government to grant loan advances to officers for specific approved purposes, subject to Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Salary may be stopped if an officer is absent from duty without leave for how long
questions.explanation: PSR 100308 provides that salary is stopped after 10 consecutive working days of absence without leave.

questions.question: Responsibility allowance ceases when:
options: Officer ceases to hold the additional responsibility.
questions.explanation: PSR 130106(d) states that responsibility allowance ends immediately an officer ceases to hold the designated responsibility.

questions.question: What authority approves the conversion of unclaimed overtime to time off
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130111(e) provides that unclaimed overtime may be converted to time off only with approval of the Permanent Secretary.

questions.question: Who authorizes the payment of hazard allowance in Ministries
options: Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130113(b) vests authority to pay hazard allowance in the Accounting Officer of the Ministry or Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 2021, salary arrears may be paid for a period not exceeding:
questions.explanation: PSR 140103(b) restricts payment of salary arrears to a period not exceeding twelve months, except with special approval.

questions.question: Which **PSR** provision deals with payment of retirement benefits
questions.explanation: PSR 090202 in Chapter 9 governs payment of pensions and gratuities as retirement benefits to eligible officers.

questions.question: Who certifies that an officer has not been overpaid before final salary payment on exit
questions.explanation: PSR 140204 assigns the Director of Finance and Accounts responsibility for certifying that officers are not overpaid before final payment.

questions.question: What **PSR** rule governs recovery of overpayment from an officer’s salary
questions.explanation: PSR 140207 provides that any overpayment to an officer shall be recovered from salary or other entitlements in line with Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Under **PSR**, no allowance shall be paid to an officer on interdiction except:
questions.explanation: PSR 100305(d) states that no allowances are payable to officers on interdiction until reinstatement.

questions.question: Salary payment to officers should not be made in advance of work except when:
options: Advance is specifically approved by Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: PSR 140102(b) prohibits payment of salary in advance unless approved by the Accounting Officer for valid reasons.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 2021, what authority reviews and updates allowances across the Public Service
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission.
questions.explanation: PSR 130100(b) empowers the National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC) to review, approve, and update all public service allowances periodically.

questions.question: In the context of Allowances, Pay & Benefits, which action best demonstrates allowances, pay & benefits governance
options: Apply approved allowances, pay & benefits procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved allowances, pay & benefits procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective allowances, pay & benefits, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure allowances, pay & benefits compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.

options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.

Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.

Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits.

questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.

questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.

questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.

options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.

options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.

Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective allowances, pay & benefits, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure appointment governance
options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on disciplinary process within Allowances, Pay & Benefits
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: When handling allowances, pay & benefits, which choice reflects proper leave administration standards
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain promotion standards in Allowances, Pay & Benefits
options: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in allowances, pay & benefits Reference: Allowances, Pay & Benefits - Expansion Set.

questions.question: What are "Junior Posts"?
options: Posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent.).

questions.question: What are "Senior Posts"?
options: Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

questions.question: Which section of the Code of Conduct addresses the issue of receiving benefits?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Section 11).

questions.question: Purpose of forwarding death-in-service documents?
options: Facilitate processing of benefits due to legal beneficiaries.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Facilitate processing of benefits due to legal beneficiaries.).

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: How are salary payments for part of a month calculated on retirement or termination of appointment?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (On a pro-rata basis.). Financial Regulation 1504 specifies that on retirement or termination of appointment, payment for part of a month must be calculated on a pro-rata basis.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that true about acting appointments?
options: They are made only when an officer is due for promotion.
They are not to be made unless absolutely necessary.
They are made for all grade levels. Q
They are only for a duration of six months.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (They are not to be made unless absolutely necessary.). Rule 020702 states that an acting appointment should "not be made unless it is necessary." Part 10: Promotion

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines a key duty of the officer controlling expenditure as per FR 1519?
options: To personally approve every single salary payment.
To guarantee the records show the officer's standard rate of payment, incremental date, allowances, and deductions.
To secure the standard personal emoluments records are kept securely.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To guarantee the records show the officer's standard rate of payment, incremental date, allowances, and deductions.). Financial Regulation 1519 details the information that the officer controlling expenditure must guarantee is shown in the standard personal emoluments records, including rate of disbursement, incremental date, and deductions

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: An acting appointment is NOT a form of trial for what role?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Promotion). Rule 020703 explicitly states, "Acting appointment is not intended as a means of testing the suitability of officers for promotion."

questions.question: Within government administration, at what point does a cessation of an acting appointment not happen?
options: When the acting officer proceeds on casual or special leave.
When the substantive holder of a duty post resumes.
When the acting officer takes up another post.
When the acting officer is promoted. Q 32
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (When the acting officer proceeds on casual or special leave.). Rule 020708 states that an acting officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties of the acting appointment when on casual or special leave70.

questions.question: According to established rules, responsibility for is accountable for forwarding departmental files and other documents necessary for processing retirement to PENCOM belongs to which role?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Departmental Pension Officer □). Rule 021003 states that "Forwarding of Departmental files and other documents not expressly mentioned in Rule 021002 must no longer be a condition for processing of retirement benefits." This implies that the Departmental Pension Officer is accountable for the overall processing. The previous rule, 021002, mentions that Departmental Pension Officers are demand to forward up-to-date records. Part 13: Certificate of Service

questions.question: Within government administration, which time frame can an officer's compulsory confirmation examination result be used for the aim of promotion? 54?
options: Only for promotion to the next grade level.
Only for promotion from junior to senior post.
Only when the officer has passed at an accelerated level. □
Never, as it is only for confirmation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Only for promotion from junior to senior post.). Rule 030502 states that the COMPRO result can be used for promotion from a junior to a senior post, subject to other conditions.

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: The fees paid to invigilators and examiners are typically separate from what other fees?
options: The invigilators' and examiners' regular salaries. □
The fees paid by the candidates for the examination.
The transport allowances of the candidates.
The fees for setting the questions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The invigilators' and examiners' regular salaries. □). Examination fees are a form of extra-duty allowance and are separate from an officer's regular salary

questions.question: In official practice, choose the option that payments is NOT exempt from Stamp Duty under all conditions?
options: Payments for goods supplied or services rendered if the amount is ₦1,500.00.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Payments for goods supplied or services rendered if the amount is ₦1,500.00.). Financial Regulation 620(b)(i) states that payments for goods supplied or services rendered are exempt if the amount is under ₦1,000.00. Therefore, ₦1,500.00 would be liable.

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What does Financial Regulation 1401 primarily address?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Personal advances). Financial Regulation 1401 explicitly deals with "Personal advances" 251

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: According to Financial Regulation 1501, what is the definition of "Salary"?
options: The personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate.
An allowance for housing and transportation
A one-time disbursement for a special project.
A bonus paid for good performance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate.). Financial Regulation 1501 defines salary as the personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate of disbursement

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of forbidden regarding salaries, allowances, and pensions?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Paying them in advance.). Financial Regulation 1503(iii) explicitly states that salaries, allowances, and pensions must not be paid in advance.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the maximum duration of an acting appointment?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (One year). Rule 020705 states that an acting appointment must not exceed one year, but in exceptional circumstances, it can be extended for another one year.

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: In official practice, choose the best answer for: What happens if an officer on an acting appointment proceeds on casual or special leave?
options: The officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment.). (The officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment.). Rule 020708 states that an acting officer "will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment" when proceeding on a casual or special leave.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the main aim of Rule 021002?
options: To access contributions to the National Housing Fund.
To provide a schedule for non-pensionable appointments.3
To workflow the retirement of an officer.
To secure officers receive their retirement benefits promptly.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure officers receive their retirement benefits promptly.). Rule 021002 states that "In order to guarantee that officers receive their retirement benefits promptly, Departmental Pension Officers are demand to forward to the Pension Commission (PENCOM) an up-to-date log of service."

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: An officer on a non-pensionable appointment must not directly or indirectly receive what?
options: Commission or profits of any kind, but will devote the whole of their time to the service of the Government.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Commission or profits of any kind, but will devote the whole of their time to the service of the Government.). Rule 021204 states that the person engaged must not "either directly or indirectly be engaged in or concerned with any other service or business whatsoever or receive commission or profits of any kind.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the chief objective of the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination for Junior Officers?
options: To confirm their appointment and/or promote them
To prepare them for promotion to senior posts
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To confirm their appointment and/or promote them). The title of the section itself, "Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examinations," indicates its dual purpose20.

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: An officer on probation who fails to pass the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination for Junior Officers is liable to what consequence?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Termination of their appointment). Rule 030104, which is a general rule for confirmation examinations, applies to all officers, including junior officers, stating that failure to pass leads to termination of appointment

questions.question: According to established rules, which office must approve any variation in the Establishment or rates of allowances approve for the Public Service?
options: The Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
Only the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service.). Financial Regulation 311 states: "Any variation in the Establishment or rates of allowances... must receive the approval of the Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service..."

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: What type of advance is mentioned in Financial Regulation 1405?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Salary advance on first appointment). Financial Regulation 1405 explicitly refers to "Salary advance on first appointment".

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: How are salaries and allowances based on an annual rate paid?
options: Monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.
As a lump sum at the end of the year. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.). Financial Regulation 1504 specifies that salaries and allowances based on an annual rate must be paid by monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.

subcategories.name: Conduct & Ethics
subcategories.description: Professional conduct and ethical obligations in service.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 2021, officers must maintain absolute loyalty to:
questions.explanation: PSR 040101 emphasizes that officers must demonstrate absolute loyalty to the Federal Republic of Nigeria and uphold the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Code of Conduct for Public Officers under the **PSR**'
options: To ensure accountability, transparency, and integrity in public service.
questions.explanation: Appendix I to PSR sets the Code of Conduct to ensure public officers act with integrity, transparency, and accountability.

questions.question: Officers must not disclose official information without authorization as stated in:
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 prohibits disclosure of official information without due authorization, even after leaving service.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule prohibits public officers from engaging in political activities
questions.explanation: PSR 040208 restricts public officers from active political activities, to maintain neutrality and objectivity.

questions.question: What is considered a breach of **PSR** 040205 regarding conflict of interest
options: Accepting a contract in which the officer has personal interest.
questions.explanation: PSR 040205 forbids officers from engaging in any business or transaction where personal interest conflicts with official duty.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040206, officers must not accept gifts or benefits except:
options: From personal friends on social occasions.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206 permits acceptance of gifts only from personal friends or relatives on non-official, social occasions.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 040209, public officers must not borrow money from:
questions.explanation: PSR 040209 forbids officers from borrowing from professional money lenders to avoid indebtedness and corruption risk.

questions.question: **PSR** 040207 prohibits officers from using their official positions to:
questions.explanation: PSR 040207 prohibits officers from using official positions to secure undue advantage for themselves, friends, or relations.

questions.question: Who has authority to investigate breaches of the Code of Conduct
questions.explanation: Appendix I to PSR and the 5th Schedule of the Constitution assign the Code of Conduct Bureau to investigate such breaches.
questions.chapter: Appendix I – Code of Conduct Bureau

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040204, officers must avoid:
options: Discrimination based on tribe, religion or gender.
questions.explanation: PSR 040204 mandates impartiality and forbids discrimination or favouritism based on religion, tribe, or gender.

questions.question: Officers must declare their assets as required by:
options: Appendix I and the 5th Schedule of the Constitution.
questions.explanation: Appendix I requires officers to declare assets as mandated by the 5th Schedule of the 1999 Constitution Reference: Appendix I – Asset Declaration.

questions.question: What should an officer do if a potential conflict of interest arises
options: Report it immediately to the Permanent Secretary.
Seek to benefit from it quietly.
Ask a colleague to act in his place.
questions.explanation: PSR 040205(b) requires officers to declare any potential conflict of interest to their superior for guidance.

questions.question: **PSR** 040210 provides that officers must avoid:
options: Indebtedness which may embarrass the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 040210 directs officers to avoid excessive indebtedness that may compromise integrity or embarrass the Service.

questions.question: Who is responsible for enforcing discipline related to ethical breaches in Ministries
questions.explanation: PSR 040103 assigns the Permanent Secretary the responsibility for enforcing discipline and ethical compliance in the Ministry.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040201, officers must demonstrate:
options: Commitment to work ethics, diligence, and integrity.
questions.explanation: PSR 040201 directs all officers to maintain diligence, integrity, and discipline in the discharge of duties.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 040211, officers who fail to comply with the Code of Conduct may face:
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 and Appendix I specify that non-compliance with the Code of Conduct may lead to dismissal or other disciplinary sanctions.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, a public officer must not accept gifts from persons:
options: Who have official dealings with him.
questions.explanation: Appendix I prohibits accepting gifts or benefits from persons or bodies having official transactions with the officer Reference: Appendix I – Gifts and Benefits.

questions.question: Which ethical principle requires officers to avoid using government property for private gain
questions.explanation: Appendix I emphasizes integrity, requiring officers to avoid using government resources for private benefit Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: What does **PSR** 040202 require regarding official correspondence
options: Must be approved by the Permanent Secretary.
Handled in accordance with laid-down procedures and confidentiality.
questions.explanation: PSR 040202 mandates that official correspondence be handled confidentially and in accordance with approved procedures.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule requires public officers to treat members of the public with courtesy
questions.explanation: PSR 040104 mandates officers to show courtesy and respect in dealing with the public.

questions.question: What should officers do when offered gifts that may appear as bribes
options: Reject and report the offer immediately.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206(b) and Appendix I require officers to reject and report such offers to the appropriate authority.

questions.question: Under Appendix I, officers must observe political neutrality and not:
options: Join political parties or attend partisan rallies.
questions.explanation: Appendix I reinforces PSR 040208, prohibiting officers from joining political parties or partisan events.

questions.question: **PSR** 040203 prohibits disclosure of:
options: Operationally, public statements by officials under normal controls.
Any information obtained during the course of official duties without authorization.
In practice, general government data in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, annual reports under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 explicitly prohibits unauthorized disclosure of official information even after retirement.

questions.question: The principle of impartiality under the **PSR** means:
options: Treating people differently based on status.
Equal treatment of all persons without bias or favour.
questions.explanation: PSR 040204 promotes impartiality, demanding equal and fair treatment of all without discrimination or bias.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, integrity in public service means:
options: Adhering to moral principles and honesty in official duties.
questions.explanation: Appendix I defines integrity as adherence to honesty, moral principles, and avoidance of corrupt practices Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: **PSR** 040105 states that every officer must discharge his duties with:
questions.explanation: PSR 040105 emphasizes honesty, impartiality and courtesy as essential values in discharging official duties.

questions.question: What action should be taken if an officer receives a gift from a contractor
options: Return and report the incident immediately.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206(b) mandates that officers return and report gifts received from individuals having official dealings with them.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040210, officers who become heavily indebted may face:
options: Disciplinary action for bringing disrepute to the Service.
questions.explanation: PSR 040210 provides that officers whose indebtedness brings embarrassment to the Service may be disciplined.

questions.question: Appendix I prohibits public officers from maintaining or operating foreign bank accounts except when:
options: They are on official posting abroad.
questions.explanation: Appendix I (Paragraph 7) allows maintenance of foreign accounts only by officers on official foreign postings Reference: Appendix I – Financial Conduct.

questions.question: Officers are prohibited from accepting property or benefit of any kind from:
options: Private companies or individuals with official dealings.
questions.explanation: PSR 040206 and Appendix I prohibit officers from accepting gifts from individuals or entities having official relations with them.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule directs officers to display political neutrality in the discharge of duties
questions.explanation: PSR 040208 provides that officers must remain politically neutral to maintain objectivity and public confidence.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, which behaviour violates integrity in public service
questions.explanation: Appendix I identifies bribery as a direct breach of the integrity principle in public service Reference: Appendix I – Integrity.

questions.question: **PSR** 040201 requires officers to uphold which of the following in performing duties
questions.explanation: PSR 040201 mandates that officers act with efficiency, honesty, and discipline at all times.

questions.question: Under **PSR** 040209, borrowing money from a subordinate is considered:
questions.explanation: PSR 040209 prohibits borrowing from subordinates or professional lenders; doing so constitutes serious misconduct.

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring officers in parastatals comply with the Code of Conduct
questions.explanation: PSR 040103 assigns each Board responsibility to enforce the Code of Conduct within its organization.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, public officers must not engage in which of the following without approval
questions.explanation: Appendix I prohibits engaging in private business except farming or other activities approved by the Head of Service Reference: Appendix I – Private Business Restriction.

questions.question: **PSR** 040203 emphasizes that information obtained in official capacity should be:
questions.explanation: PSR 040203 directs officers to use official information only for authorized purposes and maintain confidentiality.

questions.question: Which rule prohibits officers from bringing the Service into disrepute through behaviour
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 warns that behaviour bringing discredit to the Service constitutes misconduct punishable under PSR 100201.

questions.question: Appendix I requires public officers to act with transparency, which means:
options: Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability.
questions.explanation: Transparency in Appendix I requires openness, honesty, and accountability in decision-making processes Reference: Appendix I – Transparency.

questions.question: Which principle demands that public officers place public interest above personal gain
questions.explanation: Appendix I emphasizes selflessness — putting public interest above private gain — as a core ethical value Reference: Appendix I – Selflessness.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040107, officers must not engage in:
options: Operationally, religious activities under normal controls.
Procedurally, tardiness at work within applicable policy conditions.
Any activity likely to conflict with their official duties.
Under formal rules, volunteering under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 040107 restricts officers from any external activity that conflicts with their official duties or responsibilities.

questions.question: An officer who disobeys lawful instructions from a superior officer violates:
questions.explanation: PSR 040106 states that officers must obey all lawful instructions from superior officers promptly and efficiently.

questions.question: Which ethical principle is violated when an officer favours a relative in recruitment
questions.explanation: Favouring relatives breaches impartiality as defined in PSR 040204 and Appendix I.

questions.question: **PSR** 040104 emphasizes that officers must show courtesy to:
options: Members of the public and colleagues alike.
questions.explanation: PSR 040104 requires courtesy and respect to both colleagues and members of the public.

questions.question: Officers found guilty of corruption under **PSR** may face:
questions.explanation: Under PSR 100201 and Appendix I, corruption constitutes serious misconduct punishable by dismissal and possible prosecution.

questions.question: According to Appendix I, the ethical value of accountability implies that officers:
options: Must justify their decisions and actions to the public.
questions.explanation: Accountability in Appendix I means public officers must be answerable for their actions and decisions Reference: Appendix I – Accountability.

questions.question: **PSR** 040211 provides that breach of the Code of Conduct attracts:
options: Operationally, counselling only under normal controls.
Procedurally, disciplinary measures as provided in psr within applicable policy conditions.
In practice, transfer or redeployment in authorized administrative workflow.
Under formal rules, reprimand without record under established public service standards.
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 and PSR 100201 specify that breaches of the Code attract disciplinary action including dismissal.

questions.question: Which rule emphasizes that public officers must refrain from actions detrimental to government image
questions.explanation: PSR 040211 directs officers to avoid any action or speech that could discredit or embarrass the government.

questions.question: An officer who misuses confidential information for personal gain violates:
questions.explanation: Misuse of official information for personal benefit breaches PSR 040203 and the integrity provisions in Appendix I.

questions.question: According to **PSR** 040101, officers must act in accordance with:
options: The Constitution, PSR and extant regulations.
questions.explanation: PSR 040101 requires all public officers to act in conformity with the Constitution, PSR and other extant laws.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates conduct & ethics governance
options: Apply approved conduct & ethics procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved conduct & ethics procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure conduct & ethics compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on conduct & ethics risk management within Conduct & Ethics
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

options: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Apply merit-based criteria and authorized approval channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain disciplinary process in Conduct & Ethics
options: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Observe due process, fair hearing, and documented decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates leave administration
options: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Apply leave rules consistently and update personnel records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure promotion standards
options: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Confirm eligibility requirements before recommending advancement. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circular compliance within Conduct & Ethics
options: Align practice with current circular directives and PSR rules.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Align practice with current circular directives and psr rules. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics.

questions.question: When handling conduct & ethics, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Conduct & Ethics
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates public accountability
options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Conduct & Ethics
options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: When handling conduct & ethics, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Conduct & Ethics
options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Conduct & Ethics, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: For effective conduct & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure record management
options: Maintain accurate files and update status at each control point.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Maintain accurate files and update status at each control point. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in conduct & ethics Reference: Conduct & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.question: What kind of testimonial is required from applicants?
options: Good conduct from last employer or last school.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Good conduct from last employer or last school.).

questions.question: Does the Oath of Secrecy prejudice the Freedom of Information Act?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (No.).

questions.question: A public officer is expected to demonstrate what towards the public service?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Loyalty and commitment).

questions.question: A public officer who receives a gift in the course of official duties must:
options: Declare it to the appropriate authority
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Declare it to the appropriate authority).

questions.question: Which body ensures that public officers adhere to the provisions of the Code of Conduct?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Code of Conduct Bureau).

questions.question: Which constitutional document outlines the fundamental objectives of state policy, including ethical conduct?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Chapter II of the 1999 Constitution).

questions.question: The 'Oath of Allegiance' signifies commitment to:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Constitution and the nation).

questions.question: The Code of Conduct aims to prevent officers from enriching themselves at the expense of:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The state and the public).

questions.question: The Code of Conduct is primarily designed to promote:
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Good governance and public confidence).

questions.question: Which institution provides guidelines and training on ethical conduct?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Public Service Institute of Nigeria (PSIN)).

questions.question: Agency enforcing asset declaration rules?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB)).

questions.question: Prohibition on using official info for personal gain is in which instrument?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Fifth Schedule (Code of Conduct)).

questions.question: Concept requiring regular disclosure of assets and liabilities?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Code of Conduct provisions).

questions.question: Core principle of public service ethics?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Accountability and impartiality).

questions.question: Constitutional body collecting and verifying asset declarations?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB)).

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the aim of the Oath of Secrecy that some Civil Servants are demand to swear?
options: To hide personal assets from the public.
To protect government secrets and classified information. 430
To prevent them from talking to the media.
To secure they are loyal to a political party.Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To protect government secrets and classified information. 430). Officers who have access to classified information should, as soon as possible, swear to the Oath of Secrecy

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: Every newly appointed officer or staff member is demand to sign which of the following?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Oath of Secrecy). Rule 020210 mandates that every newly appointed officer/staff member must sign an Oath of Secrecy on Security (Form 1).

questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Transparency and accountability in public finance.).
