questions.question: A unit handling interpretation & commencement receives a case with competing priorities. Which action best preserves compliance and service quality' (Rule-compliant administration scenario 1)
options: Prioritize convenience over approved process requirements.
Bypass review checkpoints where timelines are tight.
Apply discretionary shortcuts to accelerate closure regardless of controls.
Apply applicable PSR provisions consistently and keep auditable records.
questions.explanation: Apply applicable psr provisions consistently and keep auditable records.

questions.question: The provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) shall be enforced by:
options: All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies).

questions.question: The Public Service Rules apply to:
options: Only officers in the Federal Civil Service
All officers in the Public Service
All civil servants except where they conflict with specific terms in their contract
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All officers in the Public Service).

questions.question: Which of the following offices is NOT explicitly mentioned in the rules?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Chief Justice of Nigeria).

questions.question: To what extent do these rules apply to the offices mentioned in 010102?
options: Only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Constitution
At the discretion of the officers
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Constitution).

questions.question: What is the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules?
options: To be aware of some of the rules
To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars
To seek clarification from their superiors
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars).

questions.question: Where are the special meanings of words and terms used in particular chapters or sections found?
options: At the end of the document
At the beginning of such chapters or sections
In the Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (At the beginning of such chapters or sections).

questions.question: What does "Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria" mean?
options: The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area
Any representative of the Nigerian government
A representative accredited to the United Nations
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area).

questions.question: What does "Civil Service of the Federation" mean?
options: Service of the Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice President, a Ministry and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation assigned with the responsibility for any business of the Government of the Federation. It is a body or organ which enjoys continuity of existence.
Contractual service with the Federal Government
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Service of the Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice President, a Ministry and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation assigned with the responsibility for any business of the Government of the Federation. It is a body or organ which enjoys continuity of existence.).

questions.question: What does "Cut-off Mark" mean?
options: The lowest score on an examination
Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.).

questions.question: What is an "Established Post"?
options: A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.
A post in any of the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.).

questions.question: Who is an "Expatriate Officer"?
options: An officer from another African country
A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.).

questions.question: What does "Gazette" mean?
options: The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.).

questions.question: Who is a "Junior Officer"?
options: An officer on GL.08 and below
A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent.
Any officer below the rank of director
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent.).

questions.question: What does "Monthly-Rated" mean?
options: Employment on monthly rates of pay.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Employment on monthly rates of pay.).

questions.question: What is "Nigerian Home Place"?
options: The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria.).

questions.question: Who is a "Nigerian Officer"?
options: An officer other than an expatriate.
An officer from a specific region
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (An officer other than an expatriate.).

questions.question: What does "Officer" mean when used without qualification?
options: Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.).

questions.question: What does "Pass Mark" mean?
options: The minimum score required for employment
The score below which a candidate is considered to have failed promotion examination which currently is 60%
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The score below which a candidate is considered to have failed promotion examination which currently is 60%).

questions.question: Who is a "Permanent Secretary"?
options: Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices. He/she also doubles as the accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister. They are appointed from among serving Directors in the Federal Civil Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices. He/she also doubles as the accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister. They are appointed from among serving Directors in the Federal Civil Service.).

questions.question: What does "Public Service of the Federation" mean?
options: The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).).

questions.question: What do "Public Service Rules" consist of?
options: Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures.).

questions.question: What is a "Scheme of Service"?
options: A welfare package for public servants

A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.).

questions.question: Who is a "Spouse of an Officer"?
options: A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.
Only a wife married under the Marriage Act
Only a husband married under Islamic Law
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.).

questions.question: What does "Staff" mean?
options: A person employed by the Federal Government.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A person employed by the Federal Government.).

questions.question: What does "Suspension" mean?
options: Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.).

questions.question: Who is a "Trainee"?
options: A person appointed to a training post in any grade; it includes "pupil".
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A person appointed to a training post in any grade; it includes "pupil".).

questions.question: How can the Federal Government amend the provisions of the Public Service Rules?
options: By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.
Through the Head of Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.).

questions.question: How are the terms 'Officer' and 'Staff' referred to in the Public Service Rules to be applied in terms of gender?
options: The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.
At the discretion of the Permanent Secretary
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.).

questions.question: What should a copy of the Public Service Rules form part of?
options: The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service.).

questions.question: Appointments to offices in the Federal Civil Service are made on the authority of:
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Federal Civil Service Commission).

questions.question: How are appointments made?
options: Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement.).

questions.question: Who is authorized to appoint eligible candidates to posts in respect of which the powers of appointment have been delegated to them?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices).

questions.question: The Federal Civil Service Commission shall make appointments to posts graded:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (GL.07-17).

questions.question: How shall appointments by the FCSC be made?
options: As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement.).

questions.question: Where shall advertisement for vacancies be placed?
options: In three national newspapers and the Commission's website.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (In three national newspapers and the Commission's website.).

questions.question: What is the deadline for submission of applications from interested candidates?
options: Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.).

questions.question: Who shall select from the pool of successful candidates at the Civil Service Examination?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Each Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office).

questions.question: Who handles the selection process in each Ministry?
options: The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF.).

questions.question: How often is the competitive Public/Civil Service Entry Examination held for posts graded GL. 07-10?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Annually).

questions.question: Who conducts the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination?
options: FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN.).

questions.question: What is the qualification requirement for the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination?
options: As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service.).

questions.question: Who makes the final recommendation for appointments?
options: The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements.).

questions.question: Who appoints Junior Staff on GL. 06 and below?
options: Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office.).

questions.question: How are appointments in Federal Offices located in the States/Zones made?
options: From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.
From other states if no local candidates qualify
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.).

questions.question: Who works out the actual establishment for each State Office?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (OHCSF.).

questions.question: Who conducts the interview to appoint the Officers (Junior Staff)?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Junior Staff Committee (Local).).

questions.question: When is the incremental date of an Officer appointed or promoted to a post?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (1st January of every year.).

questions.question: What is the condition for an Officer to enjoy the increment?
options: Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance.).

questions.question: Is the employment of unpaid Staff allowed?
options: No, the employment of unpaid Staff is prohibited.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (No, the employment of unpaid Staff is prohibited.).

questions.question: Who is responsible for keeping an up-to-date record of every Officer under their control?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretaries/Head of Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

questions.question: How should Form Gen 60 be completed?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (In ink.).

questions.question: Appointments made by the FCSC to NYSC Presidential Merit Award winners are:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Automatic regardless of vacancy constraints.).

questions.question: What percentage of recruitments shall be for persons with disability?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (5%.).

questions.question: Where posts prove difficult to fill from within the Service, they shall normally be:
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Advertised.).

questions.question: What is the normal service period for a trainee or pupil?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Two years.).

questions.question: Under what condition may the trainee period be reduced?
options: This period may however, be reduced in special circumstances.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (This period may however, be reduced in special circumstances.).

questions.question: What is the normal maximum period of secondment for training?
options: The period laid down for the training except with OHCSF special advice.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The period laid down for the training except with OHCSF special advice.).

questions.question: What is the minimum age for appointment into the Federal Public Service?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (18 years.).

questions.question: What is the maximum age for appointment into the Federal Public Service?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (50 years.).

questions.question: What is one of the qualifications every applicant must possess?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Computer proficiency/literacy.).

questions.question: Who must certify an applicant as medically fit?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (An authorized Healthcare Provider.).

questions.question: What is required for an Officer to be appointed?
options: Authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from OHCSF.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from OHCSF.).

questions.question: If previously dismissed, what is required for a new appointment?
options: Prior specific approval of FCSC or Board/Council.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Prior specific approval of FCSC or Board/Council.).

questions.question: What must every applicant for government employment state?
options: Whether they have been convicted of a criminal offence.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they have been convicted of a criminal offence.).

questions.question: What must an applicant state about previous employment?
options: All employment they have engaged in.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All employment they have engaged in.).

questions.question: What must an applicant disclose if they have left any employment?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Why they did so.).

questions.question: What must an applicant state if they are still in any employment?
options: Whether they are under any obligation to remain in it.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they are under any obligation to remain in it.).

questions.question: What must an applicant declare regarding financial status?
options: Whether they are free from financial embarrassment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they are free from financial embarrassment.).

questions.question: Which form is to be completed by applicants for senior posts?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (FCSC Application Form (FC.2).).

questions.question: Who processes FC.2 forms and publishes shortlists?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Commission (FCSC).).

questions.question: Which form shall accompany Gen. 69C?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Form No. Gen. 60.).

questions.question: When shall an Officer enter Personal Particulars on Gen. 60?
options: Immediately a newly appointed Officer assumes duty.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Immediately a newly appointed Officer assumes duty.).

questions.question: What is the duty of every Permanent Secretary regarding the Oath of Secrecy?
options: Ensure all newly employed sign an Oath of Secrecy (Form 1) and that Oaths are preserved.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure all newly employed sign an Oath of Secrecy (Form 1) and that Oaths are preserved.).

questions.question: Can a public Officer be a member of a secret society?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (No.).

questions.question: What should an Officer who is a member of a secret society before appointment do?
options: Renounce membership forthwith by statutory declaration.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Renounce membership forthwith by statutory declaration.).

questions.question: What is the maximum probationary period allowed without extension?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Three years.).

questions.question: For clerical grade Officers, what is the prescribed examination?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Promotion/Advancement Examination for clerical officers.).

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring conditions for confirmation are met?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.).

questions.question: What is the general principle governing advancement and promotion?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Merit, experience, and ability.).

questions.question: What is the minimum period spent on a grade to be considered for promotion (General Rule)?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Two years.).

questions.question: What is the exception to the minimum period requirement?
options: Accelerated promotion exercises approved by FCSC.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Accelerated promotion exercises approved by FCSC.).

questions.question: What is the primary method of promotion?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Written examination and interview.).

questions.question: Who is responsible for issuing the result of the promotion examination?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Federal Civil Service Commission.).

questions.question: Under what circumstances can an Officer act in a higher post?
options: Substantive holder absent for prolonged period or temporary vacancy.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Substantive holder absent for prolonged period or temporary vacancy.).

questions.question: Maximum period an Officer can act in a higher post without special approval?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Six months.).

questions.question: What does "transfer" mean?
options: Permanent movement from one Ministry to another of same status or grade.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Permanent movement from one Ministry to another of same status or grade.).

questions.question: Can an Officer be transferred without consent?
options: Yes, but normally only after consultation with officer and MDAs.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Yes, but normally only after consultation with officer and MDAs.).

questions.question: What are "inter-cadre transfers"?
options: Movement from one cadre to another on the same grade.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Movement from one cadre to another on the same grade.).

questions.question: Who approves inter-cadre transfers?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission.).

questions.question: What is the maximum period of secondment without review?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Two years.).

questions.question: What is "posting"?
options: Assignment of an Officer to a duty station within his Ministry.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Assignment of an Officer to a duty station within his Ministry.).

questions.question: Public officers must not put themselves in a position where their personal interest conflicts with their:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Official duties).

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Code of Conduct for Public Officers?
options: Ensure high standards of public morality and accountability
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure high standards of public morality and accountability).

questions.question: Which of the following is a core value expected of a public officer?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity).

questions.question: Public officers are forbidden from engaging in any private business, trade, or profession EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Farming).

questions.question: What is the consequence for a public officer who fails to declare assets as required?
options: Sanctions by the Code of Conduct Tribunal
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Sanctions by the Code of Conduct Tribunal).

questions.question: The principle of 'public trust' in public service implies that public officers should:
options: Act solely in the interest of the public
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Act solely in the interest of the public).

questions.question: What is the maximum number of times a public officer can apply for an extension to declare assets?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Once).

questions.question: The concept of 'due diligence' in public service refers to:
options: Exercising reasonable care and investigation before taking action
Making decisions based on personal biases
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Exercising reasonable care and investigation before taking action).

questions.question: Public officers should avoid situations that could give rise to:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The appearance of impropriety).

questions.question: The principle of 'accountability' means public officers are answerable to:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The public and relevant authorities).

questions.question: What is the ethical implication of using government property for personal use?
options: Breach of the Code of Conduct
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Breach of the Code of Conduct).

questions.question: Public officers are expected to show 'impartiality', meaning they should:
options: Treat all citizens equally without bias
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Treat all citizens equally without bias).

questions.question: The concept of 'transparency' in public service requires public officers to:
options: Be open and clear about actions and decisions
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Be open and clear about actions and decisions).

questions.question: What is the role of the CCT regarding public officers who breach the Code?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Adjudicate and impose sanctions).

questions.question: Public officers are prohibited from operating foreign bank accounts to prevent:
options: Money laundering and illicit financial flows
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Money laundering and illicit financial flows).

questions.question: What is the ethical obligation regarding confidential information?
options: Keep it confidential and use it only for official purposes
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Keep it confidential and use it only for official purposes).

questions.question: The principle of 'objectivity' means decisions should be based on:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Facts and merits, not feelings).

questions.question: What is the ethical responsibility regarding public funds?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Manage them efficiently and accountably).

questions.question: The principle of 'public interest' implies that public officers should:
options: Act solely in the interest of the public
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Act solely in the interest of the public).

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the ethical implication of using government property for personal use?
options: Breach of the Code of Conduct
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Breach of the Code of Conduct). (Breach of the Code of Conduct).

questions.question: Public officers are expected to show 'impartiality', meaning:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Treat all citizens equally).

questions.question: Transparency in public service requires officers to:
options: Be open and clear about decisions
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Be open and clear about decisions).

questions.question: What is the role of the CCT regarding breaches?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Adjudicate and impose sanctions).

questions.question: In cases involving fraud or dishonesty, which agency must be notified?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (ICPC).

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Code of Conduct under the PSR?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure accountability, transparency, and integrity).

questions.question: Which ethical principle requires avoiding using government property for private gain?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity).

questions.question: Which behaviour violates integrity in public service?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Receiving bribes for official favours).

questions.question: Transparency in Appendix I means:
options: Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability).

questions.question: The ethical value of accountability implies that officers:
options: Must justify their decisions and actions to the public
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Must justify their decisions and actions to the public).

questions.question: The term Audit Trail refers to documentation of financial transactions to ensure:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity and traceability, ensuring accountability).

questions.question: Primary agency for financial crimes like fraud and money laundering?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (EFCC).

questions.question: Which body monitors compliance and institutional corruption in public finance?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (ICPC).

questions.question: Agency responsible for receiving and verifying public officers’ asset declarations?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (CCB).

questions.question: Chief Accounting Officer personally responsible for funds within an MDA?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Permanent Secretary).

questions.question: Documentation of financial and procurement transactions ensuring integrity and traceability?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Audit Trail).

questions.question: Consequence for a bidder attempting to influence the evaluation committee?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Blacklisting from future federal tenders).

questions.question: Required step if a bidder's offer is non-responsive?
options: Notify BPP immediately and exclude the bid
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Notify BPP immediately and exclude the bid).

questions.question: Why is the Permanent Secretary ultimately responsible for procurement compliance?
options: Designated the Chief Accounting Officer for the MDA
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Designated the Chief Accounting Officer for the MDA).

questions.question: System ensuring integrity and traceability throughout the procurement cycle?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Audit Trail).

questions.question: Body monitoring anti-corruption and institutional ethical standards (excluding financial crimes)?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (ICPC).

questions.question: PSR Chapter providing foundation for integrity and accountability?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Chapter 15).

questions.question: Mandatory frequency for asset declaration?
options: Every four years, and upon assumption of duty
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Every four years, and upon assumption of duty).

questions.question: Loss of funds must be communicated to which three offices?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Police, Auditor-General, and Accountant-General).

questions.question: Agency investigating financial crimes and money laundering?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC)).

questions.question: Loss of funds prompt notification offices?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Police, Auditor-General, and Accountant-General).

questions.question: Mechanism documenting financial transactions chronologically?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Audit Trail).

questions.question: Audit Trail ensures:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All transactions are chronologically traceable).

questions.question: Purpose of Servicom Dashboards?
options: Citizen feedback and service quality metrics
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Citizen feedback and service quality metrics).

questions.question: Agency for digital fraud in the public sector?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (EFCC).

questions.question: Vital leadership principle for maintaining ethical standards?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Accountability).

questions.question: Integrity and Accountability are essential for which cadre?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Leadership (Directorate and above)).

questions.question: A Directorate leader ensures fund utilization follows which Chapter of CSH?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Chapter 4).

questions.question: Audit Trail refers to sequential documentation to ensure:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity and traceability).

questions.question: Primary objective of NSPSR?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Enhance capacity, efficiency, and accountability).

questions.question: NSPSR aims to build a public service that is:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (More efficient, effective, and accountable).

questions.question: Role of M&E in implementing national strategies?
options: Track progress, assess impact, and ensure accountability
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Track progress, assess impact, and ensure accountability).

questions.question: Ethical consideration in negotiation?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Building trust and maintaining integrity).

questions.question: What is 'program budgeting'?
options: Linking expenditures to programs and intended outcomes
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Linking expenditures to programs and intended outcomes).

questions.question: What is 'results-based planning'?
options: Emphasizes defining clear outcomes, outputs, and impacts
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Emphasizes defining clear outcomes, outputs, and impacts).

questions.question: Primary objective of Freedom of Information Act?
options: Ensure public access to information held by public institutions
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure public access to information held by public institutions).

questions.question: FOI Act enhances governance by promoting transparency and:
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Facilitating accountability).

questions.question: Public Complaints Commission enhances:
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Administrative accountability and citizen redress).

questions.question: What is "resignation"?
options: Voluntary termination of appointment by the Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Voluntary termination of appointment by the Officer.).

questions.question: Who has the power to dismiss an Officer?
options: FCSC or Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office as the case may be.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (FCSC or Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office as the case may be.).

subcategories.name: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline
subcategories.description: Official circular updates on appointments, tenure and discipline.

questions.question: The Tenure Policy Circular limits the tenure of career Directors (**GL 17**) and Permanent Secretaries to a maximum of:
questions.explanation: The Tenure Policy Circular (reaffirmed in 2016 and 2017) limits the tenure of Permanent Secretaries and Directors to a maximum of eight years to ensure succession and renewal Reference: Tenure Policy.

questions.question: The 2017 Circular on Tenure Review emphasized the strict adherence to the 8-year limit for Directors primarily to achieve:
options: Succession planning and avoidance of career stagnation.
Reduction in staff strength across MDAs.
Exemption of technical staff from retirement.
questions.explanation: The 2017 Circular reaffirmed strict adherence to the 8-year tenure limit for Directors to prevent stagnation and facilitate succession planning Reference: Tenure Enforcement.

questions.question: Under the 2013 Circular, what is the minimum consecutive period an officer must serve in an acting appointment before qualifying for the payment of acting allowance
questions.explanation: The 2013 OHCSF Circular clarified that an officer must act for a minimum of 14 days before qualifying for the payment of acting allowance.

questions.question: Which body retains the primary authority for discipline over officers on the established cadre (**GL 07** and above), as reinforced by the 2015 Disciplinary Circulars
options: The Office of the Head of Service (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The 2015 Circular on disciplinary procedures reaffirmed that disciplinary powers for officers on the established cadre remain vested in the FCSC Reference: Discipline and Conduct.

questions.question: If an officer’s transfer is at their own request, extant circulars (based on **PSR** **Rule 140137**) clarify they are entitled to:
questions.explanation: An Officer whose transfer is at his own request shall be entitled to only transport allowance and not resettlement allowance Reference: Allowances on Transfer.

questions.question: Under delegated authority, who is authorized to promote eligible candidates to delegated posts on SGL 06 or its equivalent and below
questions.explanation: Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices are authorized to promote eligible candidates to delegated posts on SGL 06 or its equivalent and below Reference: Delegated Authority.

questions.question: The period of secondment to an approved body or recognized international organization shall not exceed:
questions.explanation: The period of secondment shall not exceed six years Reference: Secondment.

questions.question: An officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination (COMPRO) after three consecutive attempts is required to:
options: Resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Proceed on compulsory leave without pay.
questions.explanation: Failure after three consecutive attempts requires the officer to resign or be withdrawn from the service Reference: Confirmation Examination.

questions.question: Who is responsible for notifying the Federal Civil Service Commission (**FCSC**) of cessation of acting appointments using Form No. Gen. 15A
questions.explanation: Ministries shall be required to notify the FCSC when officers revert from acting appointment by completion of Form No. Gen. 15A Reference: Acting Appointments.

questions.question: The duration of probation for first appointments shall not exceed two years unless an extension is approved by the:
questions.explanation: The period of probation shall not exceed two years unless an extension is approved by the FCSC Reference: Probationary Period.

questions.question: An officer may be interdicted or suspended if their work or conduct results in adverse comments. The objective of conveying such reports in 'sympathetic terms' is to encourage the officer to overcome their:
questions.explanation: The objective of reporting adverse comments in sympathetic terms is enabling and encouraging him to overcome his shortcomings Reference: Performance Feedback.

questions.question: The **PSR** strictly prohibits an officer from undertaking any private agency in any matter connected with:
options: The exercise of his public duties.
questions.explanation: No Officer shall undertake any private agency in any matter connected with the exercise of his public duties Reference: Conduct and Ethics.

questions.question: Who must approve the release of any Officer on study leave, whether paid or unpaid, upon the recommendation of the Officer’s Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The release of any Officer on study leave shall be subject to the approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation on the recommendation of the Officer’s Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office Reference: Study Leave Approval.

questions.question: Who is responsible for maintaining and forwarding staff records, including the approved Staff Records Form, to the Pension Fund Administrator/National Pension Commission
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Director (HRM) is responsible for ensuring staff records are completed and submitted to the Pension Fund Administrator/National Pension Commission Reference: HR Records Management.

questions.question: An officer on an incremental grade level shall normally be granted an increment unless he is on interdiction/suspension or:
options: Has completed the compulsory COMPRO exam.
questions.explanation: An increment is withheld or deferred if the officer is on interdiction/suspension or has a disciplinary action pending Reference: Emoluments and Discipline.

questions.question: The deferment period for an officer's increment must be fixed and must not be less than three months nor more than:
questions.explanation: The deferment period must not be less than three months nor more than six months Reference: Deferred Increment.

questions.question: Which officer holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment, requiring assessment of service improvement and conduct
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment Reference: Increment Restoration.

questions.question: For effective circulars: appointments, tenure & discipline, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure circulars: appointments, tenure & discipline compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in circulars: appointments, tenure & discipline Reference: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on circulars: appointments, tenure & discipline risk management within Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in circulars: appointments, tenure & discipline Reference: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set.

questions.question: According to established rules, which role is accountable for making acting appointments for a duty post below SGL 14?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
This is not explicitly stated in the provided text.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (This is not explicitly stated in the provided text.). Rule 020701 only specifies that acting appointments are made for a duty post not lower than SGL

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: To whom is the Certificate of Service issued?
options: Only to officers on GL. 07 and above.
All officers, including those on non-pensionable appointments, on leaving the service.
Only to officers on GL. 06 and below. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (All officers, including those on non-pensionable appointments, on leaving the service.). Rule 021101 states that "all officers, including those of non-pensionable appointments, must be issued, on leaving the Service, a certificate of service.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which role is necessitate to countersign the Certificate of Service for officers on GL. 06 and below?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Permanent Secretary/Career Management Officer (CMO)). Rule 021102 states that for officers on GL. 06 and below, the certificate "demand the counter-signature of the Permanent Secretary/Career Management Officer (CMO). Part 14: Schedule for Non-Pensionable Appointment

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What rank must a liaison officer designated by each ministry or agency for pension matters not be below?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Director. 11). Financial Regulation 1904(e) specifies that each ministry or agency must designate a liaison officer not below the rank of a Director. 282

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: What does the Code of Ethics aim to achieve in the Civil Service?
options: To continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction.
To reduce the number of government ministries. Q.
To provide guidelines for political appointments.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction.). The handbook states that in order to continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction, the requisite Code of Ethics should be embraced by its members

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the tenure for an intern or a volunteer in the public service?
options: Not more than 12 months in a full-time academic session
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Not more than 12 months in a full-time academic session). Rule 020418 states that interns must be engaged for 3 to 6 months on a part-time basis during an academic session and for a period of not more than 12 months on a full-time basis38.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: For an officer posted outside their professional cadre, what is the mandatory period they should serve in such a post?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Four years). Rule 020602(i)(b) stipulates that posting of officers to posts outside their professional cadres is prohibited53. However, rule 020602 (i) (c) says that posting of pool officers in the service must be mandatory every four (4) years.

questions.question: In the public service context, which office is accountable for submitting a report on compliance with posting instructions to the relevant pool office within three weeks of the release of the instruction?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Director of Human Resources (DHR)). Rule 020605 states that the "Director of HR must be expected to submit a report on compliance to posting instructions to the relevant pool office within three weeks of the release of the instruction."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: which role is accountable for the countersignature of the Certificate of Service for officers holding senior posts?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the Federal Civil Service Commission.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the Federal Civil Service Commission.). Rule 021102 states that the Certificate of Service is "to be issued to Officers holding senior posts on GL.07 and above and necessitate the counter-signature of the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the Federal Civil Service Commission.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the timing for an officer on a non-pensionable appointment to express their desire for further employment?
options: Four months before the date of expiration of their agreement.
Three months before the date of expiration of their agreement.
One month before the expiration of their agreement.
Six months before the date of expiration of their agreement. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Four months before the date of expiration of their agreement.). Rule 021212 states that the person engaged must give a written notice to the government stating their desire to remain in employment "four months before the date of expiration of agreement.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the processing period for cash transfers in respect of AIEs issued by ministries/extra-ministerial offices in favor of state offices, in view of the electronic banking system?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Not exceed seven (7) working days.). Financial Regulation 721(i)(c) states: "the processing period ofcash transfers in respect of AIE's issued by ministries/extra ministerial offices and other arms of government in favour of state offices must not exceed Seven (7) working days."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence if an officer negligently disregards the provisions of Regulation 719 when accepting a cheque that is subsequently dishonored?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Surcharge for the full amount.). Financial Regulation 731 states: "An officer accepting a cheque which is subsequently dishonoured must be surcharged for the full amount, if the provisions Of Regulation 719 were negligently disregarded."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: select the option that best answers: What types of transfers are effected by Principal Journal Vouchers (PJVs) at the end of the month?
options: Transfers related to revenue collection only.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Transfers from "Above-the-line Accounts" to "Below-the-line Accounts."). Financial Regulation 904(ii)(c) states that PJVs are prepared to "effect month-end transfer of accounts from Above-the-line Accounts to Below-the-line Accounts."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the minimum period of time a deposit must remain unclaimed before it is transferred to revenue?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Three years). Financial Regulation 1311 states that if the money has remained on deposit for more than three years, it will be transferred to revenue.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes one of the responsibilities of the National Pension Commission regarding accounting records?
options: To delegate all accounting responsibilities to ministries.
To destroy accounting records after five years.
To sustain accounting records for contributions and secure proper periodic reconciliation.
To only report to the Auditor-General. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To sustain accounting records for contributions and secure proper periodic reconciliation.). Financial Regulation 1904(c) states that the Commission must keep accounting records for the contributions and secure proper periodic reconciliation of the accounts.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: select the option that best answers: What must all Accounting Officers secure is created in their ministries/extra-ministerial offices regarding pensions?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A Subsidiary Account Pension Unit.). Financial Regulation 1905 states that all Accounting Officers must secure that a Subsidiary Account Pension Unit is created.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: How should the Subsidiary Account Pension Unit duty in relation to the main subsidiary accounts section of ministries?
options: It should be integrated with the main section.
It should be supervised by the main accounts section
It should report directly to the Accountant-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (It should duty independently. 15). Financial Regulation 1905 states that the section must duty independently of the main subsidiary accounts section.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: What type of account is the Retirement Savings Account (RSA)?
options: A suspense account for unclaimed funds.
An investment account managed by ministries. □
An account specific to each employee for pension contributions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (An account specific to each employee for pension contributions.). Financial Regulation 1904(a) states the NPC will open "Retirement Savings Accounts (RSA) for each employee."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the consequence for an Accounting Officer who fails to appear before the Public Accounts Committee when summoned?
options: A penalty of two years' imprisonment
The committee can recommend sanctions, including dismissalQ.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The committee can recommend sanctions, including dismissalQ.). Failure of an accounting officer to appear before the committee when summoned is a serious offense, and the committee can recommend sanctions, including dismissal.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: Below the Permanent Secretary, who heads the Departments and is directly accountable to the Permanent Secretary?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Directors). Below the Permanent Secretary are the other cadres of Civil Servants. Directors head the Departments and are directly accountable to the Permanent Secretary.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: To whom do the provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) apply?
options: Only to officers on grade level 17 and above.
Strictly to all ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and agencies.
Only to the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
To all employees in the private sector
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Strictly to all ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and agencies.). Rule 010101 states, "The provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) must be strictly enforced by all Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices, and Agencies."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the typical period of probation for an officer newly appointed to the service?
options: Not less than six months and not more than two years.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Not less than six months and not more than two years.). Rule 020301 specifies that officers on probation are demand to serve for two years, but this period can be reduced to not less than six months or extended up to two years.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: Which body is in charge for making recommendations regarding the confirmation of officers holding senior posts?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office). Rule 020304 states that recommendations for the confirmation of officers holding senior posts must be made by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: A newly recruited officer who has spent six months and above must be allowed to sit for compulsory examinations for confirmation in the service. Which rule specifies this?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (020306). Rule 020306 specifies that a newly recruited officer who has spent six months and above must be allowed to sit for compulsory examinations for confirmation.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: An officer's probationary appointment is terminated or extended based on the recommendation of which authority?
options: The Federal Civil Service Commission Q
The authority empowered to appoint the officer
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The authority empowered to appoint the officer). Rule 020303 indicates that at the end of the probationary period, the officer will, unless their probationary appointment is terminated or extended, be confirmed in their appointment by the authority empowered to appoint them29.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Appointments, Tenure & Discipline - Expansion Set

subcategories.name: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances
subcategories.description: Official circular updates on leave, welfare, and allowances.

questions.question: Which Establishment Circular formally approved **Paternity Leave** for male officers in the Nigerian Public Service
options: 2014 Circular on Leave Computation and Paternity Leave.
questions.explanation: The 2014 OHCSF Circular on 'Computation of Leave Based on Working Days and Approval of Paternity Leave' formally introduced paternity leave for male officers Reference: Leave and Welfare.

questions.question: Under the 2014 Circular, computation of annual leave days is based on which calendar basis
options: Working days, excluding weekends and public holidays.
questions.explanation: Leave computation was adjusted to working days, excluding weekends and public holidays, for fairness and clarity Reference: Leave Administration.

questions.question: The 2015 Establishment Circular directed that all **MDAs** must approve and submit their annual leave rosters by which quarter of the year
questions.explanation: All MDAs were directed to submit approved leave rosters by the first quarter to enable effective planning and control of staff absences Reference: Leave Administration.

questions.question: The 2011 Circular on staff welfare mandated the establishment of which committee in **MDAs** to oversee staff wellbeing
questions.explanation: The 2011 Circular mandated the creation of Welfare Committees to oversee staff wellbeing and morale within MDAs Reference: Staff Welfare.

questions.question: The 2013 Circular on Staff Loans directed that loan applications must be supported by what document for verification
questions.explanation: The Circular required that staff loan applications be accompanied by the applicant's most recent pay slip for verification Reference: Staff Welfare and Loans.

questions.question: The 2012 Circular on Duty Tour Allowance (DTA) revised rates primarily based on what determining factor
questions.explanation: The revised duty tour allowance rates were based on officers' grade levels to ensure equity and reflect inflation trends Reference: Duty Tour Allowances.

questions.question: Which specific body in the Federal Public Service reviews allowances and makes recommendations based on circulars initiated by the **OHCSF**'
options: The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
The Budget Office of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC) reviews allowances in the Federal Public Service Reference: Allowance Review.

questions.question: The maximum sick leave period before a Medical Board examination is mandatory, according to the **PSR**:
questions.explanation: If an officer is still not fit for duty after three months (90 days) sick leave, examination by a Medical Board shall be required Reference: Medical Welfare.

questions.question: Which category of leave practice is explicitly listed in the **PSR** (and consequently eliminated in operational circulars) as abolished
questions.explanation: Deferred Leave is listed as one of the types of leave that is abolished Reference: Leave Administration.

questions.question: If an officer receives more leave during the leave year than they are eligible for, they will be required to refund the excess salary based on the number of:
options: Working days’ leave enjoyed in excess.
questions.explanation: The officer shall be required to refund salary calculated at the rate it was paid for the number of working days’ leave enjoyed in excess Reference: Leave Forfeiture.

questions.question: Officers who are going on religious pilgrimages, other than Officials assigned to cover such activities, should utilize which leave type
questions.explanation: Officers going on religious pilgrimages should use part of their annual leave for such purposes Reference: Leave Utilization.

questions.question: An officer who is dismissed from the service shall forfeit all claims to retiring benefits, leave, or transport grant, subject to the provisions of the:
questions.explanation: Dismissal results in forfeiture of benefits, subject to the provisions of the Pension Reform Act, 2014 (as amended).

questions.question: The **PSR** mandates that all allowances, such as House Master/Mistress allowance or Science Teaching Allowance, shall be paid at the rates specified in which official document
questions.explanation: Allowances are paid at the rates specified in the Extant Circular Reference: Allowances.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration of study leave with pay that may be granted to an Officer
questions.explanation: The duration of study leave with pay shall not exceed two years Reference: Study Leave.

questions.question: The length of vacation leave granted to an Officer on invalidation (medically unfit to continue service) shall be two months and will commence:
options: Six months before the official retirement date.
On the day the Medical Board declares him unfit.
On the first day of the following calendar year.
questions.explanation: The two months leave granted upon invalidation commences on the day the Medical Board declares the Officer permanently unfit for service Reference: Medical Invalidation.

questions.question: If an officer on vacation leave is recalled to duty by the Permanent Secretary, the officer is entitled to resume the unexpired portion of his leave when
options: Operationally, immediately upon return to the office under normal controls.
On the day he ceases to hold a substantive post or act in another post in the same station.
In practice, after three months of continuous service in authorized administrative workflow.
Only at the discretion of the FCSC.
questions.explanation: The unexpired portion of his leave may be resumed on the day he ceases to hold a substantive post, or act in another post in the same station.

questions.question: For effective circulars: leave, welfare & allowances, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure circulars: leave, welfare & allowances compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in circulars: leave, welfare & allowances Reference: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set.

options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in circulars: leave, welfare & allowances Reference: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set.
