questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence if a driver fails to keep the logbook up to date? 288?
options: No consequence, as long as the journey was official.3
Forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.). Financial Regulation 2010 states that failure to keep the logbook up-to-date will result in the forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: which role is in charge for prescribing the dates in the month on which salaries and allowances must be paid?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accountant-General. Q). Financial Regulation 1505 states that the Accountant-General must prescribe the dates for salary and allowance payments each month. 258
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: How long does the Sub-Accounting Officer have to disbursement unclaimed salaries, allowances, and pensions to the Treasury?
options: At the end of the financial year.
As soon as the cash is withdrawn.a 259
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Within seven days.). Financial Regulation 1513(i) specifies that unclaimed salaries, allowances, and pensions must be paid to the Sub-Accounting Officer within seven days from the date of withdrawal
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What document provides the legal framework for the investment of pension funds by the Central Bank of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Pension Reform Act.). Financial Regulation 1904(b) states that investments are made "as provided in the Pension Reform Act."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: The Board of a Parastatal is expected to be financially in charge for what?
options: The day-to-day expenses of the Parastatal.
The management of the Parastatal's bank accounts.a..
The preparation of the Parastatal's budget.
The honorarium and allowances of the Board members.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The preparation of the Parastatal's budget.). The Board is expected to prepare the Parastatal's budget for submission to the National Assembly through the supervising Ministry.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the mandatory training period for newly appointed Foreign Service Officers at the Foreign Service Academy?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (One year.). All new Foreign Service Officers must attend a mandatory one-year training course in diplomatic practice at the Foreign Service Academy.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: choose the best answer for: A sabbatical appointment is applicable to which grade level?
options: GL. 10 or equivalent and above Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (GL. 15 or equivalent and above). Rule 020413 states that sabbatical appointment applies to "the Service at GL. 15 (or equivalent) and above."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the period of notice for relinquishing an appointment during a probationary period?
options: A month's notice or disbursement of one month's salary in lieu of notice.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A month's notice or disbursement of one month's salary in lieu of notice.). Rule 020902 states that an officer may relinquish their appointment by giving "one month's notice, or by paying one month's salary in lieu of notice."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: COMPRO I is for officers on what grade levels?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (GL08 and GL 09). The syllabus section of the document specifies that COMPRO I is for officers on grade levels 08 and 09, and COMPRO II is for officers on grade levels 10-14
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: The payment of fees to invigilators and examiners is typically based on what?
options: A flat rate for all examinations.
The grade level of the officers they are examining. □
The number of questions they set.
The number of candidates they supervise or papers they mark.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The number of candidates they supervise or papers they mark.). While not explicitly detailed, examination fees are conventionally tied to the work performed, such as marking papers or invigilating candidates, which is a standard practice in similar contexts
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: The Schemes of Service are issued and revised to indicate which of the following?
options: The amount of pension to be paid to retired officers. □.
The disciplinary procedures for all civil servants.
The number of staff to be recruited each year.
The correct nomenclature, salary grade levels, qualifications for entry, and schedule of duties for each cadre.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The correct nomenclature, salary grade levels, qualifications for entry, and schedule of duties for each cadre.). The Schemes of Service indicate the correct nomenclature, the lowest to the highest Salary Grade Levels, qualifications for entry and progression within the cadre, and the 421 schedule of duties at each Grade Level.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: which office should be necessitate to explain why they are late to work?
options: All staff who signed below the red line.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All staff who signed below the red line.). Late-comers should be necessitate to explain why they are late. This applies to all staff who signed below the red line.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars?
options: To delegate the duty to subordinates.
To challenge them if they disagree
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To acquaint himself with them.). Rule 010103 states, "It must be the duty of every officer to acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What happens to an officer who leaves the service on the grounds of failing a compulsory examination?
options: The officer is eligible to reapply immediately.
The officer is given another chance to take the exam.
The officer will not be regarded as having been dismissed from the service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The officer will not be regarded as having been dismissed from the service.). Rule 020906 states that an officer who fails a compulsory examination "must not be regarded as having been dismissed from the service."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the role of issuing a Certificate of Service?
options: To provide a document of an officer's salary.
To provide a log of an officer's leave.
To provide a summary of the officer's performance.3
To be used as a reference, covering the holder's Public Service when seeking other employment. 40
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To be used as a reference, covering the holder's Public Service when seeking other employment. 40). Rule 021103 states that the main objective of issuing a Certificate of Service is that it "may be used as a reference, covering the holder's public service when seeking other employment."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: For an officer to be confirmed in their appointment, they must pass the prescribed examination within how many years?
options: As specified by the Examination Board E3
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Two years). Rule 020301 specifies that the probationary period for an officer is two years, within which they must pass the examination to be confirmed
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: A Supplementary General Warrant empower funds for additional services approved in which document?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Supplementary Estimates.). Financial Regulation 305 states: "A Supplementary General Warrant authorises the Accountant-General to issue funds to disbursement for the additional personal emoluments and other services which must have been approved in Supplementary Estimates..."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which time frame must all standing imprests be retired?
options: When the cash advance holder goes on leave.
Within one month of the issue date.
On or before 31st December of the financial year in which they are issued.
Only upon the explicit instruction of the Accountant-General. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (On or before 31st December of the financial year in which they are issued.). Financial Regulation 1011(i) states: "All standing imprests must be retired on or before the 31st December of the financial year in which they are issued..."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the duty of Sub-Accounting Officers regarding receipt and licence books?
options: To guarantee their safe custody and proper use.
To dispose of them after use.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To guarantee their safe custody and proper use.). Financial Regulation 1130 states, "Sub-Accounting Officers must be in charge for the safe custody and proper use of all receipt and licence books issued to them." 239 Financial Regulations: Receipt and Licence Books
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: Question: If a cashier or officer temporarily leaves receipt books with another officer, who is held accountable for any misuse?
options: The cashier or officer who left the receipts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The cashier or officer who left the receipts.). Financial Regulation 1212(iii) clearly states, "Such cashiers or officers who leave the receipts so issued to them with another officer on a temporary basis must be held accountable for any misuse."
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: What happens to interest earned on bank accounts, according to Financial Regulations?
options: It must be properly classified to the appropriate revenue head and paid to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
It can be used for departmental welfare.
It can be retained by the unit.
It is transferred to a suspense account. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It must be properly classified to the appropriate revenue head and paid to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.). Financial Regulation 222 (from a related chapter but a general principle) states that interest earned on bank accounts must be properly classified and paid to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: What type of register must be maintained by the Head of the Internal Audit Unit?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A progress register.). Financial Regulation 1705 demand the Head of the Internal Audit Unit to sustain a "progress register" to document the status of work done by the audit staff.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the proper procedure for a Civil Servant who has been transferred to a new office?
options: They should take all the files with them to their new office.
They should leave immediately without any handover.
They should ask their new supervisor to get the files. □.
They should submit a formal handing-over note.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They should submit a formal handing-over note.). An officer being transferred to a new office must prepare a formal handing-over note to their successor.
questions.chapter: Circulars: Leave, Welfare & Allowances - Expansion Set

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the best way to destroy classified documents that are no longer needed?
options: Tear them up and throw them in the waste bin.
Leave them on a desk for others to see.
Put them in the Open Registry. □. 454
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Shred them or burn them.). Unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper shredding machine or burnt.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the best way to destroy classified documents that are no longer needed?
options: Leave them on a desk for others to see.
Tear them up and throw them in the waste bin.
Put them in the Open Registry.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Shred them or burn them.). Unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper shredding machine or burnt.

questions.question: According to the 2015 Establishment Circular and **PSR** **Rule 040102**, which platform was mandated for salary and personnel management across all **MDAs**'
questions.explanation: The 2015 OHCSF Circular made the Integrated Personnel and Payroll Information System (IPPIS) mandatory for all MDAs to ensure payroll integrity Reference: Personnel Management.

questions.question: All newly employed Officers must be placed on the **IPPIS** platform within a maximum period of:
options: Two months of assumption of duty.
questions.explanation: All Officers shall be placed on the IPPIS platform within two months of assumption of duty (Rule 040102).

questions.question: The 2016 Circular on Biometric Capture mandated which activity for all public officers
options: Completion of biometric registration for identity verification.
questions.explanation: The 2016 OHCSF Circular on biometric integration mandated all public officers to complete biometric registration to ensure identity verification Reference: HR Modernization.

questions.question: The 2016 Circular on Performance Management introduced which modern HR evaluation tool to replace the Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER)
questions.explanation: The 2016 OHCSF Circular launched the Performance Management System (PMS) to replace the APER and align staff goals with organizational objectives Reference: Performance Management.

questions.question: Which Establishment Circular reinforced **SERVICOM principles** to promote efficiency and citizen-focused service
questions.explanation: The 2011 Circular on Service Delivery reinforced SERVICOM principles to promote efficiency and citizen-focused service Reference: Service Delivery and Reforms.

questions.question: Who is primarily responsible for cascading the Performance Management System (PMS) framework throughout their respective **MDAs** for full implementation
options: The Head of the Civil Service.
questions.explanation: Permanent Secretaries were tasked with cascading the PMS framework throughout their MDAs for full implementation Reference: PMS Implementation.

questions.question: The **PSR** incorporated provisions for **virtual meetings** primarily to reflect what modern necessity
options: Enable remote work continuity during pandemics/emergencies.
questions.explanation: Virtual meetings were introduced to enable remote work continuity during pandemics/emergencies, supporting uninterrupted government business Reference: E-Governance.

questions.question: Who issues Treasury Circulars that provide detailed instructions on financial procedures and implementation, complementing the **PSR** and **FR**'
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General of the Federation issues Treasury Circulars Reference: Financial Administration.

questions.question: The purpose of the three months **pre-retirement leave** required of officers who give notice of retirement is primarily for:
options: To allow them to apply for transfer.
To attend workshops and prepare documentation for retirement benefits.
questions.explanation: The officer attends workshops and prepares documentation during the three months pre-retirement leave Reference: Retirement Procedures.

questions.question: The implementation of the Reward and Recognition Scheme (R&RS) is intended to encourage excellence, merit, and to ensure high levels of staff:
questions.explanation: The implementation of the R&RS System encourages excellence, merit, and ensures high levels of staff motivation Reference: Reward and Recognition.

questions.question: The Public Service Awards Ceremony, mandated under the R&RS, shall be held how often to recognize service-wide awardees
questions.explanation: The Public Service Awards Ceremony shall be held annually Reference: Reward and Recognition.

questions.question: Who is statutorily designated as the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government, coordinating fiscal circulars and policy
questions.explanation: The Minister of Finance is the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government Reference: Financial Management.

questions.question: The compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination (COMPRO) must be passed by eligible officers within how many years from the date of taking up their appointments
questions.explanation: Officers must pass the confirmation examination within two years from the date of taking up their appointments Reference: Confirmation Examination.

questions.question: If an officer on **GL 14** meets all criteria, the minimum number of years required on the grade level before being considered for promotion to **GL 15** is:
questions.explanation: An officer on GL 14 can be considered for promotion after spending a minimum of 3 years on the grade (Rule 020802(c)).

Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: The fees for invigilators and examiners are for:?
options: All examinations conducted in the public service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Only the compulsory confirmation examinations.). This section is specifically titled "Fees for Invigilators and Examiners" under the chapter on "Prescribed Examination for Confirmation," indicating its scope.

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that a role of the Board of a Parastatal concerning personnel?
options: To be the sole disciplinary body for all staff.
To manage the day-to-day recruitment of junior staff.
To manage the payroll and salaries. □.
To approve the appointment and promotion of staff.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To approve the appointment and promotion of staff.). The Board is accountable for the approval of the appointment and promotion of staff.

questions.question: which role determines the seniority of an officer?
options: The date of appointment or the date of assumption of duty, whichever is earlier and duly certified in the appropriate register.
The date of assumption of duty
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The date of appointment or the date of assumption of duty, whichever is earlier and duly certified in the appropriate register.). Rule 020105 states that seniority is determined by the date of assumption of duty certified by an empower officer and/or date of present appointment as reflected in the appropriate register and career progression.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the consequence of failing to pass the compulsory confirmation examinations for clerical grade officers?
options: The officer may have their appointment terminated at the end of the probationary period.
The officer will have their probationary period extended.
The officer will be demoted. a
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer may have their appointment terminated at the end of the probationary period.). Rule 020303 specifies that an officer on probation is necessitate to pass the prescribed examination, and failure to do so may lead to the termination of the appointment

options: The Civil/Public Service and Private institutions.

The Federal Civil Service Commission. a

options: The officer may have their appointment terminated at the end of the probationary period.
The officer will have their probationary period extended.

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: For a probationary officer, how often should the compulsory confirmation examinations be held?
options: As stipulated by the Examination Board Q 46
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Once every six months). Rule 030106 specifies that these examinations must be held at intervals of not more than six months.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option professional officers are exempted from passing the compulsory confirmation examination?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Law professionals □). Rule 030109 grants an exemption from the compulsory confirmation examination to law professionals.

questions.question: In official practice, select the statement that materials are allowed to be used by candidates during the compulsory confirmation examinations?
options: Reference books specified by the Examination Board.
All Public Service Rules and Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence for an officer who fails to present themselves for a compulsory confirmation examination without an acceptable reason?
options: The officer is given another chance to write the exam.
The officer's appointment is liable to be terminated.
The officer is suspended for a month.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer's appointment is liable to be terminated.). According to Rule 030105, an officer who fails to present himself/herself for the examination without an acceptable reason is liable to have their appointment terminated. Part 16: Compulsory Confirmation Examinations for Senior Executive Officers, Administrative Officers and Professionals (COMPRO I & II)

options: Neither, as they are senior officers. □

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the maximum number of sittings an officer is allowed for the compulsory confirmation examination?

Yes, they must sit for both.

questions.question: In the public service context, responsibility for is accountable for establishing and supervising Federal payment Offices in each state capital of the federation belongs to which role?

questions.question: In official practice, which role is personally in charge for ensuring that expenditure against a sub-head from which provision was transferred by a virement warrant does not exceed the reduced balance?
options: Officers authorised to incur expenditure (officers controlling votes).
The Head of Finance and Accounts
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Officers authorised to incur expenditure (officers controlling votes).). Financial Regulation 317 states: "Officers controlling votes are personally in charge for ensuring compliance with this regulation."

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: If a serious irregularity in a voucher is established or suspected, what is the first action the officer controlling the vote should take?
options: Immediately investigate and report the circumstances.
Report to the Minister of Finance.
Transfer the funds to a suspense account.Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Immediately investigate and report the circumstances.). Financial Regulation 409 states: "Where a serious irregularity in a voucher is established or suspected the officer controlling the vote must immediately investigate and report the circumstances..."

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What happens if expenditure is incorrectly charged to a vote?
options: It will be transferred to a general fund.
It will be adjusted in the next financial year.
It will be reported to the Minister of Finance for approval. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It will be disallowed.). Financial Regulation 417 states: "Expenditure incorrectly charged to a vote must be disallowed."

questions.question: In official practice, which office is accountable for the appointment of the Internal Auditor?

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: The existence of "ghost workers" is symptomatic of what problem in the Civil Service?
options: Inadequate linkages between training and duty posts.
Poor personnel records and a flawed payroll control system.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Poor personnel records and a flawed payroll control system.). The existence of ghost workers is symptomatic of poor personnel records and a flawed payroll control system, particularly in MDAs not yet part of the IPPIS programme.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: Which two key institutions have oversight responsibilities for policy articulation and implementation, as well as quality assurance on Human Resources issues?

The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) and the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Ministry of Finance and the Budget Office of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) and the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).). questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: According to the Civil Service Handbook, what is a chief element in good governance?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Service delivery). The handbook states that service delivery is a chief element in good governance, and efficient and effective delivery of good service is the raison d'etre of government.
